multiplechoiceresponse.label: How does the structure of the audit tree support Lecture 2’s claim that fairness is improved at a small cost to meritocracy?

choicegroup.choice: The tree shows that flips are distributed evenly across all feature values
The tree demonstrates that the optimized model achieves the highest possible accuracy
The tree reveals that flips are limited to specific subgroups rather than applied broadly
The tree indicates that protected attributes dominate all decision rules

div.p: Fairness is achieved through targeted adjustments rather than sweeping changes to outcomes. When the audit tree shows flips concentrated in specific regions of the feature space, it indicates that only a small subset of borderline cases were altered. This preserves the original merit-based structure for most individuals while correcting disparities. As a result, demographic parity is improved with only a limited impact on meritocracy.
